name: Lauren De Young
story_relevance_note: AZ Republic's named transportation and Tempe city government reporter, ASU journalism alum. Owns this beat exactly: Tempe pavement, transit, council coverage.
personalization_hook: Saw your April 12 piece on Arizona Department of Transportation construction in AZCentral.[1]

name: Senna James
beat: Tempe police, breaking news, campus safety
story_relevance_note: Bylined the Dec 2025 fatal Tempe Tavern collision article. Already covering Tempe street incidents involving ASU students.
personalization_hook: Saw your article "Tempe PD says underage student was at Tempe Tavern before fatal..." in The State Press on the December 2025 hit-and-run collision.[1][6]

name: Sophia Bracci
story_relevance_note: Reported on Tempe streetcar fare implementation Aug 2025. Active on Tempe transit beat.
personalization_hook: Saw your article "Tempe streetcar will now require fare to ride" in The State Press on the streetcar's new $1 fare implementation.[1]

name: Emma Alvarez
story_relevance_note: January 2026 byline on Tempe-ASU road closures. Direct match for jurisdictional pavement story.

name: Ally Lillestol
story_relevance_note: December 2025 byline on ASU student parking garages. Covers campus transportation infrastructure.
personalization_hook: Saw your December 2025 piece on ASU student parking garages in The State Press.[1]

name: Emma Bradford
story_relevance_note: Authored April 2025 long-form Insight piece on Arizona's most dangerous highway. Already framed around AZ road danger and statistics.
personalization_hook: Saw your Insight piece on the trials and tribulations of parking in Lot 59.[2]

name: Andi Ruiz
story_relevance_note: November 2025 byline on Tempe development and council action. Plugged into Tempe city government decisions affecting students.
personalization_hook: Saw your November 2025 piece on Tempe's new mixed-use development replacing the abandoned Food City near ASU.[1]

name: Katie Boligitz
story_relevance_note: President of TBAG. Authored 2026 Tempe council candidate questionnaire on bike issues. Directly engaged on Tempe pavement and bike infrastructure.
personalization_hook: Saw your "2026 City Council Election Questionnaire" on Tempe bike issues.[1][2]

name: Ignacio Delgadillo
story_relevance_note: TBAG VP. TBAG issued public statement on Oct 2024 15th St & College Ave crash. Group is active on exactly the Tempe streets in this story.
personalization_hook: Saw your group's Statement on 15th St & College Ave Crash from October 7, 2024.[1]

name: Hannah Moulton Belec
story_relevance_note: TBAG Treasurer. Advocacy group officer with public email handles civic campaigns on Tempe street safety.
personalization_hook: Saw your reporting on the NCAA's influx of new college hockey players eligible due to recent eligibility changes.[3]

name: Andrew Platt
story_relevance_note: TBAG Secretary, multi-year board member. Long institutional memory on Tempe-ASU bike route condition.

name: Stevie Milne
story_relevance_note: Former TBAG President, current rides director. Rides Tempe streets weekly and tracks pavement conditions firsthand.

name: Emma Lodes
story_relevance_note: Runs TBAG social channels. Amplifies Tempe street-condition stories to engaged local cycling audience.

name: Phoenix Spokes People
story_relevance_note: Phoenix metro cycling advocacy nonprofit. Maintains public Advocacy Cheat Sheet directing cyclists to report infrastructure failures. Direct fit for jurisdictional confusion angle.
personalization_hook: Saw your signing onto the letter of support for the Vision Zero Road Safety Action Plan.[2]

name: Sara Anderson
story_relevance_note: Named staff at the statewide AZ bicycling coalition. Covers Tempe and Phoenix metro and weighs in on AZ legislation affecting cyclists.
personalization_hook: Saw your work on the April survey and travel stipend initiative for Arizona bicyclists.

name: Corey Woods
story_relevance_note: Sitting Tempe Mayor. Tempe pavement near ASU and the Motorized Mobility Subcommittee fall directly under his administration.
personalization_hook: Saw your work leading the Mill Avenue Refresh, Downtown Tempe’s first major infrastructure project in 40 years.[2]

name: Berdetta Hodge
story_relevance_note: Tempe Councilmember up for re-election 2026. Pavement, scooter rules, and Notice-of-Claim exposure are live issues for her constituents.

name: Randy Keating
story_relevance_note: Tempe Councilmember historically vocal on transit and bike infrastructure. Likely sponsor or supporter of Motorized Mobility Subcommittee work.
personalization_hook: Saw your work on the Federal and State Advocacy Review Council Subcommittee on transportation and sustainability, announced at the Dec. 11, 2025 Work Study Session.[3]

name: Disability Rights Arizona
beat: Statewide AZ disability rights, ADA accessibility
story_relevance_note: AZ's federally designated Protection and Advocacy agency. Pavement gaps, sidewalk failures, and inaccessible street design at ASU campus are squarely in their docket.
personalization_hook: Saw your work on the Client Assistance Program (CAP) advocating for vocational rehabilitation rights under the Rehabilitation Act of 1973.[2]

name: Ability360 Advocacy
story_relevance_note: Phoenix-based AZ disability nonprofit. Cumulative-injury and accessible-route angle on Tempe pavement near ASU directly maps to their advocacy beat.
